title: Conference Room Port Dead After IP Phone Swap

ticket.summary: The conference room 2A wall port stopped working this morning right after facilities swapped the old IP phone for a new one. The link LED blinked briefly and went dark; neither the new phone nor a laptop plugged into the same jack gets any connectivity now. Other conference rooms are fine.

topology.description: Conference room 2A's wall jack terminates on sw-fl2-acc1 Gi0/7, configured as an access port with port-security limiting the port to one specific MAC address (statically configured for the old phone during a hardening initiative last year). The violation mode is 'shutdown', which err-disables the port on a violation.

logs.content:
Jul 17 07:48:31: %PORT_SECURITY-2-PSECURE_VIOLATION: Security violation occurred, caused by MAC address 7cad.749b.2210 on port GigabitEthernet0/7.
Jul 17 07:48:31: %PM-4-ERR_DISABLE: psecure-violation error detected on Gi0/7, putting Gi0/7 in err-disable state
Jul 17 07:48:32: %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface GigabitEthernet0/7, changed state to down
Jul 17 07:48:33: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/7, changed state to down

cli_outputs.output:
Port      Name               Status       Reason               Err-disabled Vlans
Gi0/7     ConfRoom-2A        err-disabled psecure-violation

Port Security              : Enabled
Port Status                : Secure-shutdown
Violation Mode             : Shutdown
Maximum MAC Addresses      : 1
Total MAC Addresses        : 1
Configured MAC Addresses   : 1
Last Source Address:Vlan   : 7cad.749b.2210:150
Security Violation Count   : 1

interface GigabitEthernet0/7
 description ConfRoom-2A
 switchport mode access
 switchport voice vlan 150
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security mac-address 3c5a.b012.88f4
 switchport port-security violation shutdown
end

monitoring.notes: The err-disable dashboard shows a single psecure-violation event on sw-fl2-acc1 Gi0/7 at 07:48 — exactly when facilities connected the replacement phone. The statically-secured MAC on the port (3c5a.b012.88f4) belongs to the decommissioned phone; the new phone's MAC (7cad.749b.2210) triggered the violation. No other ports affected.

hints: The link came up briefly and then went hard down with no connectivity for anything plugged in — check whether the switch put the port in a special disabled state.
'show interfaces status err-disabled' and the syslog %PM-4-ERR_DISABLE line tell you the reason; then compare the secured MAC in the port-security config with the new phone's MAC.

Port-security on Gi0/7 statically allows only the old phone's MAC (3c5a.b012.88f4); the new phone (7cad.749b.2210) triggered a psecure-violation and the port went err-disabled. Update the secured MAC and bounce the port (shutdown/no shutdown) to recover.

solution.root_cause: Gi0/7 has port-security with a statically configured MAC for the old phone (3c5a.b012.88f4) and violation mode 'shutdown'. When the replacement phone (7cad.749b.2210) was connected, it triggered a psecure-violation and the switch err-disabled the port, killing all connectivity on the jack.
solution.fix: Remove or update the static secured MAC on Gi0/7 to the new phone's address (or switch to sticky learning), then recover the port with 'shutdown' / 'no shutdown' (or errdisable recovery). Consider violation mode 'restrict' for user-facing ports to avoid hard outages on device swaps.
